free_text: Investigation of the 23Na(p,gamma)24Mg and
23Na(p,alpha)20Ne reactions via (3He,d) spectroscopy
20-MeV 3He2+ beam was provided by the
Triangle Universities Nuclear Laboratory FN tandem
accelerator, with typical beam currents between 100
and 150 pnA.
Two targets of NaBr, 49 and 102 microg/cm2. Third
target consisting  of 16 microg/cm2 metallic sodium.
The outgoing deuterons and 3He were
momentum-analyzed with the TUNL Enge Split-Pole
Spectrometer and detected using a 42-cm long
position-sensitive avalanche counter. The target was
monitored using a DE-E silicon telescope, mounted in
the target chamber at 44.2 degrees.
The solid angle of the spectrometer was
fixed at 2.0 msr in order to reduce the widths of the
contaminant lines arising from carbon, nitrogen and
oxygen in the target.

The thermonuclear reaction rate is Na <sv>,
where Na is Avagadros number and <sv> is the
thermally-averaged product of total cross section and
velocity (in the center of mass). The contribution
from an isolated, narrow resonance at Ec.m. depends
on the resonance strength. For situations where the
tails of resonances are important, or if the
resonances are broad, <sv> must be evaluated
numerically. The indirect determination by the
(3He,d) reaction.
